info.parenttitle: A3.3 Introduction to mechanical systems (HL only)

markdown:
## Motion in Mechanical Systems


1. **Motion** is a fundamental concept in **mechanical systems**, enabling machines to perform tasks by converting energy into movement. 
2. There are four basic types of motion:
  1. **Linear**
  2. **Rotary**
  3. **Oscillating**
  4. **Reciprocating**

### Linear Motion


<definition term="Linear Motion">Movement in a **straight line**, in one direction or back and forth.</definition>

<callout type="example">* **Conveyor Belts**: Transport goods in a straight path
* **Rail Systems**: Trains move along tracks in a linear direction
* **Hydraulic Pistons**: Convert fluid pressure into straight-line movement</callout>

![A train moves long a track in a linear motion (Arriva)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/1a9ca021939107f60970c2f87cc04785ca9347c1-2833x1597.png)

### Rotary Motion


<definition term="Rotary Motion">Movement around a **circular path** or axis.</definition>

<callout type="example">* **Wheels**: Enable vehicles to move by rotating around an axle
* **Gears**: Transfer motion and force between components
* **Turbines**: Convert fluid flow into rotational energy</callout>

![A bicycle wheel is an example of rotary motion, where the rotation of the wheel propels the bike forward (family can travel)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/d46f032fce28f2c6843523c57e1fb68d81840253-1024x768.png)

### Oscillating Motion


<definition term="Oscillating Motion">Movement **back and forth along a curved path**.</definition>

<callout type="example">* **Pendulum Clocks**: Use oscillation to regulate time
* **Windshield Wipers**: Move back and forth to clear rain from a windshield
* **Seesaws**: Oscillate around a central pivot</callout>

![A pendulum demonstrates oscillating motion, swinging back and forth in a regular rhythm due to the force of gravity and inertia (Saint Mary's University)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/639c3c1afb17f80189a76cf801f9f09a88a915d1-2004x2298.png)

### Reciprocating Motion


<definition term="Reciprocating Motion">Repetitive **back-and-forth** movement in a **straight line**.</definition>

<callout type="example">* **Internal Combustion Engines**: Pistons move up and down to convert fuel into mechanical energy
* **Sewing Machines**: Needles move in a reciprocating motion to stitch fabric
* **Air Compressors**: Pistons compress air through reciprocating movement</callout>

![A scroll saw operates with a reciprocating blade motion, ideal for precise and intricate cuts (einhell)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/57e74b839f63240a71cb9f6ef0b5de6e69ef55b4-1310x832.png)

###


![Comparing types of motion.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/25bfa563350f531ccb552fede43029210eaa034e-5493x1809.png)

<callout type="self_review">In small groups, explore the **DT workshop and surrounding areas** to find **real-life examples** of each of the four types of mechanical motion:

* Linear
* Rotary
* Oscillating
* Reciprocating</callout>

<callout type="warning">* **Confusing oscillating with reciprocating motion: **remember oscillating is curved, reciprocating is straight.
* Mixing up **linear** and **reciprocating:** linear is usually one-directional or back and forth with constant motion, while reciprocating often reverses direction rhythmically.</callout>
